diagrams.title: Fork-Join Execution Model
diagrams.description: Visual representation of OpenMP's fork-join parallel execution model showing sequential execution, parallel region with thread spawning, concurrent execution, synchronization barrier, and return to sequential execution.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrate how OpenMP creates parallel execution from sequential code and the synchronization points inherent in the model.

diagrams.title: Data Sharing Attributes in Parallel Regions
diagrams.description: Diagram showing how different variables are treated in an OpenMP parallel region - shared variables accessible by all threads, private variables having independent copies per thread, and reduction variables being private then combined.
elements: Shared Variables (single copy, all threads access)
Original Variable (preserved after region for private/firstprivate)
diagrams.purpose: Clarify the memory semantics of different data sharing clauses and their effects on variable lifecycle.

diagrams.title: Cache Line False Sharing
diagrams.description: Illustration of false sharing showing two threads modifying different variables (a and b) that happen to reside on the same cache line, causing cache invalidation traffic despite no actual data dependency.
elements: Cache Line containing Variable A and Variable B
Write to A triggers invalidation of line
Subsequent write to B requires reloading cache line
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate why performance can degrade dramatically when threads access different variables that share cache lines.
